title: Tomoyuki Kaneko
text: Home * People * Tomoyuki Kaneko Tomoyuki Kaneko [1] Tomoyuki Kaneko , a Japanese computer scientist, and associate professor at Graduate School of the University of Tokyo . His research interests include machine learning in games , and automated feature construction for evaluation functions of general game players . He is co-author of the open source Shogi program GPS Shogi [2] , available under GPL version 2 or later. In April 2013, GPS Shogi, running on a computer cluster of 700 PCs in the University of Tokyo, beat Hiroyuki Miura, one of the Top-10 professional Shogi players [3] [4] .
